title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. africana
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. africana, also known as the African marsh orchid or bog orchid, is a subspecies of terrestrial orchid native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce swelling, relieve pain, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
